---
title: "What to Watch from AI Engineer World's Fair 2026"
author: "Lawrence Wu"
date: '2026-07-09'
categories: [ai, conference, youtube, agents]
---

AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 just wrapped in San Francisco, and the [AI Engineer YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@aiDotEngineer/videos) has started posting talks. I wanted a quick way to decide what to watch first, so I pulled the current video metadata and ranked the posted talks by views per day. Note this post and all of the code associated with grabbing the data was written by GPT 5.5 (High) using the Codex harness.

The raw view count alone is a little unfair because the uploads are staggered. A talk posted on June 9 has had a month to accumulate views; a talk posted on July 8 has had about a day. So the main ranking below is:

```text
views per day = views / max(1, days since upload)
```

As of July 23, 2026 at 6:36 AM Pacific, the early winners are:

1. [Why Agentic Systems Need Ontologies — Frank Coyle, UC Berkeley](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sir59K8ZDPU)
2. [Field Guide to Fable — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9fubhllmsBU)
3. [Understanding is the new bottleneck — Geoffrey Litt, Notion](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WkBPX-oDMnA)
4. [Everything we knew about software has changed — Theo Browne, @t3dotgg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xUnRQ9vLXxo)
5. [The Golden Age of AI Engineering — Alexander Embiricos & Romain Huet & Peter Steinberger, OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pMggiOb18tc)

## Method

I included 142 posted conference-talk videos from June 8, 2026 onward. I excluded the 2026 vibe reel, the "6 Things to Know" preview, and scheduled premieres that did not have public view counts yet.

The topic and summary fields are lightweight notes derived from the title, not transcript-level summaries. The popularity ranking is the reproducible part: YouTube metadata, upload date, views, and views per day.

AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 ran June 29 to July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco, according to the [conference schedule](https://www.ai.engineer/schedule).

## Ranked Watchlist

| Rank | Video | Topic | Summary | Views | Views/day |
|---:|---|---|---|---:|---:|
| 1 | [Why Agentic Systems Need Ontologies — Frank Coyle, UC Berkeley](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sir59K8ZDPU) | Ontologies for agents | Frank Coyle argues that agentic systems need explicit ontologies to reason reliably over structured knowledge. | 8,110 | 8,110.0 |
| 2 | [Field Guide to Fable — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9fubhllmsBU) | AI-native product design | A guide to Fable and the product/design patterns behind building with modern AI systems. | 117,287 | 6,899.2 |
| 3 | [Understanding is the new bottleneck — Geoffrey Litt, Notion](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WkBPX-oDMnA) | Code understanding | Argues that understanding what AI-written systems do, not producing code, is the new limiting factor. | 85,165 | 6,551.2 |
| 4 | [Everything we knew about software has changed — Theo Browne, @t3dotgg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xUnRQ9vLXxo) | AI product strategy | Theo Browne frames what is worth building now that AI has changed developer workflows and product expectations. | 82,681 | 5,512.1 |
| 5 | [The Golden Age of AI Engineering — Alexander Embiricos & Romain Huet & Peter Steinberger, OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pMggiOb18tc) | AI engineering keynote | OpenAI leaders discuss why this is a golden age for engineers building with frontier models. | 71,792 | 5,128.0 |
| 6 | [Claude for Long-Horizon Tasks — Lance Martin, Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9QebvrrY3KY) | Long-horizon agents | Lance Martin covers patterns for keeping Claude on track across long, multi-step tasks. | 5,022 | 5,022.0 |
| 7 | [Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNzCG3lw6O0) | Agent skills | Practical guidance for designing reusable skills that make agents more capable and dependable. | 112,138 | 4,672.4 |
| 8 | [Every company should have a Brain — Garry Tan, Y Combinator](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eBUyTS7SzV4) | AI business strategy | Garry Tan argues that AI is rewriting the fundamental economics and dynamics of building a business. | 27,518 | 4,586.3 |
| 9 | [Don't Ship Skills Without Evals — Philipp Schmid, Google DeepMind](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0vphxNt4wyk) | Skill evals | Philipp Schmid argues that agent skills need real evaluation coverage before they ship to production. | 39,643 | 4,404.8 |
| 10 | [From fork() to Fleet: Designing an Agent Sandbox Cloud — Abhishek Bhardwaj, OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OqM67QG_Ikk) | Agent sandboxing | Abhishek Bhardwaj describes designing a cloud sandbox system for safely running many agent processes at scale. | 37,021 | 3,702.1 |
| 11 | ["The engineer of the future is the person who is able to choose what is worth doing." — Addy Osmani](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n97BCfyFIvw) | Engineering judgment | Addy Osmani argues that choosing what is worth building matters more than raw execution speed. | 31,830 | 3,536.7 |
| 12 | [HTML Is All Agents Need — James Russo, HeyGen](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cz4v1WHVyZc) | Agent-generated graphics | Argues HTML is the right substrate for agents to produce visual and interface output. | 6,518 | 3,259.0 |
| 13 | [In the Land of AI Agents, the Verifiers Are King — Tariq Shaukat, Sonar](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VrpEyglYgeU) | Agent verification | Makes the case that verification, not generation, is the decisive capability for reliable agents. | 7,395 | 2,465.0 |
| 14 | [CrabRAG: Why Automated Assistants Need Graph Memory, Not More Tokens — Stephen Chin, Neo4j](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q0VkgCyNVUg) | Graph memory for RAG | Stephen Chin argues automated assistants need graph-based memory rather than ever-larger token windows. | 2,416 | 2,416.0 |
| 15 | [Your agent architecture has a half-life of 6 months — Dan Farrelly, CTO, Inngest](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X1kp-ABIIxQ) | Agent architecture | Argues agent architectures decay fast and must be designed to be replaced as the field moves. | 4,155 | 2,077.5 |
| 16 | [Every Harness Will Become A Claw — Sam Bhagwat, Mastra](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8qWIPUia2O8) | Agent harnesses | Argues every agent harness is converging toward a Claw-like general-purpose tool. | 3,438 | 1,719.0 |
| 17 | [Stop Burning Tokens: Why self-improvement needs domain expertise first - Annabell Schäfer, Langfuse](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eAXxdtNlK04) | Self-improving agents | Argues agent self-improvement needs domain expertise first before it can stop wasting tokens. | 8,434 | 1,686.8 |
| 18 | [State of the Union: Why Local, Why Now — NVIDIA, Osmantic, Roboflow, EXO Labs, @matthew_berman](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KB41dTlX1Uc) | Local AI | A multi-vendor panel on the case for running AI locally rather than in the cloud. | 20,013 | 1,667.8 |
| 19 | [The Great Loops Debate — Dex Horthy, Geoff Huntley, Ian Livingstone, Greg Pstrucha, @insecure-agents](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c35YoMdnI78) | Agent loop design | A panel debates how the control loops driving AI agents should be structured. | 9,913 | 1,652.2 |
| 20 | [Thinner Agents on a Smarter Substrate: The Ontology-based Semantic Layer — Emil Eifrem, Neo4j](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VGN22pPpb-8) | Ontology semantic layer | Emil Eifrem makes the case for thinner agents built on a smarter, ontology-based semantic substrate. | 1,563 | 1,563.0 |
| 21 | [Forward Deployed Engineering at Cursor — Pauline Brunet](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=APqXGyCoGW4) | Forward-deployed engineering | Pauline Brunet describes how forward-deployed engineers work directly alongside customers at Cursor. | 14,049 | 1,561.0 |
| 22 | [The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=spNAUEgq_A8) | Domain agents | Argues for agents specialized around concrete domains rather than generic assistants. | 36,910 | 1,537.9 |
| 23 | [From Writing Code to Designing Systems: How the Developer Role is Changing — Chris Noring, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GdvKNwMcfd0) | Developer role shift | Discusses how the developer's role is moving from writing code to designing broader systems. | 17,137 | 1,428.1 |
| 24 | [Your Moat Is Your Data Model — Mike Phipps, Gates Foundation](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jt1Pbr_n6oU) | Data model as moat | Mike Phipps argues a company's data model, not its models or prompts, is its durable competitive moat. | 1,412 | 1,412.0 |
| 25 | [AI’s Jurassic Park Period — Aaron Stanley, dbt Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1lgFGaHoGq8) | AI risk | Compares the current AI moment to Jurassic Park's overreach and draws out the lessons. | 4,106 | 1,368.7 |
| 26 | [2026 State of AI Engineering — Barr Yaron, Amplify Partners](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGe6EjucbzI) | State of AI engineering | A data-driven survey of where AI engineering stands in 2026. | 2,702 | 1,351.0 |
| 27 | [Special Topics in Kernels, RL, Reward Hacking in Agents — Daniel Han, Unsloth](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uIiA6DquRiE) | RL and reward hacking | Daniel Han covers kernels, reinforcement learning, and reward-hacking pitfalls in training agents. | 7,755 | 1,292.5 |
| 28 | [A Practitioner's Guide to Graphs - Tim Ainge, Good Collective](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ySF0I5iE_0) | Graphs for AI | A practitioner's guide to using graph structures in AI applications. | 5,981 | 1,196.2 |
| 29 | ["Software engineering is not about writing code" — Benoit Schillings, Google DeepMind VP of Research](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1P1hJ36rxM0) | Engineering judgment | Benoit Schillings argues software engineering is about problem-solving, not writing code. | 7,093 | 1,182.2 |
| 30 | [WTF Is the Context Layer? The Missing Infrastructure for Production Agents — Prukalpa Sankar](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8G_1-3IO4ZQ) | Context infrastructure | Prukalpa Sankar defines the context layer as the missing infrastructure production agents actually need. | 10,338 | 1,148.7 |
| 31 | [Active Graph Agent Runtime (BabyAGI 4) — Yohei Nakajima, Untapped Capital](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=khVX_BUnEwU) | Graph agent runtime | Yohei Nakajima introduces an active graph-based agent runtime in the latest iteration of BabyAGI. | 1,143 | 1,143.0 |
| 32 | [Claude Fable, Claude Tag, and Anthropic's Culture — Cat Wu & Thariq Shihipar ft Simon Willison](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uU5Gv2h8-9g) | Fireside conversation | Simon Willison talks with Anthropic's Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar about building with AI today. | 8,784 | 1,098.0 |
| 33 | [Build Evals That Actually Matter - Nick Ung & Akshay Sharma, Lyft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3z2uT5aDx_Y) | AI evals | Nick Ung shares how to design evaluations that meaningfully measure agent quality instead of vanity metrics. | 4,371 | 1,092.8 |
| 34 | [Why We Killed Our Multi-Agent Pipeline — Subbiah Sethuraman and Abhilash Asokan, ZS Associates](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u6jJcIFDLE4) | Multi-agent lessons | A candid account of why ZS Associates scrapped their multi-agent pipeline and what replaced it. | 1,026 | 1,026.0 |
| 35 | [The Desktop Frontier — Ahmad Osman, Osmantic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XV2oYi7kojc) | Desktop AI | Explores running capable AI on the desktop as the next frontier. | 2,025 | 1,012.5 |
| 36 | [Recursive Model Improvement — Lee Robinson, Cursor, SpaceXAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4Tr-DknG2M) | Recursive model improvement | Lee Robinson discusses using models to recursively improve themselves and the tools around them. | 6,487 | 810.9 |
| 37 | [We Cut 94% of AI Coding Tokens With a Local Code Index - Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dRmWYHuIJxM) | Code indexing | Shows how local code indexing can sharply reduce token usage in AI coding workflows. | 18,747 | 749.9 |
| 38 | [Modern Post-Training: A Deep Dive  — Will Brown, Prime Intellect](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V-EDrhIhHzQ) | Open AI infrastructure | Will Brown presents the Prime Intellect stack for open, decentralized AI training and infrastructure. | 7,233 | 723.3 |
| 39 | [The UX of AI: Making AI-Powered Apps Your Users Don't Hate - Kathryn Grayson Nanz, Progress Software](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L3RuP_q8Bwc) | AI UX design | Offers UX guidance for building AI-powered apps that users actually enjoy using. | 3,519 | 703.8 |
| 40 | [Imagination Engineering: "Live in the future and then build what's missing."](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z2Erdirpudo) | AI product design | Eve Bouffard explores how AI expands what designers can imagine and prototype. | 4,887 | 698.1 |
| 41 | [Better Agent Auth — Bereket Habtemeskel & Paola Estefania, Better Auth](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JvKO40CFq-s) | Agent authentication | Covers authentication patterns purpose-built for AI agents. | 1,386 | 693.0 |
| 42 | [Citation Needed: Provenance for LLM-Built Knowledge Graphs — Daniel Chalef, Zep AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H7puB0RwJMM) | Knowledge-graph provenance | Daniel Chalef covers tracking provenance and citations in LLM-constructed knowledge graphs. | 604 | 604.0 |
| 43 | [What if the harness mattered more than the model? - Aditya Bhargava, Etsy](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2e9ANoOEn28) | Evaluation harnesses | Makes the case that the surrounding harness can matter more than model choice for real results. | 9,558 | 597.4 |
| 44 | [HTML is All You Need (for Agents to Make Graphics) - Amol Kapoor, Nori](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JRTAtZ5iBkU) | Agent-generated graphics | Argues that HTML is a strong substrate for agents producing visual outputs. | 13,747 | 549.9 |
| 45 | [From Systems of Record to Systems of Context — Omri Bruchim & Tomer Ast, monday.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Btk8wDUVs74) | Systems of context | Argues software is shifting from systems of record to systems of context built for AI. | 532 | 532.0 |
| 46 | [From Blind Spots to Merged PRs: Continuous Agentic Performance Optimization - May Walter, Hud](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JJGbw4ggaFs) | Agentic optimization | May Walter covers continuously optimizing agent performance from blind spots through to merged pull requests. | 2,083 | 520.8 |
| 47 | [Why Your Agent Disagrees With Itself (And What To Do About It) - Diane Lin, Datadog](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wEc9aG7cRQc) | Agent consistency | Diane Lin examines why an agent can contradict its own earlier answers and how to make its behavior consistent. | 1,540 | 513.3 |
| 48 | ["I've never seen anything scarier than an LLM with tool calls." — Erik Meijer aka @HeadinTheBox](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-CnA2lGfymY) | Formal verification for agents | Erik Meijer argues for grounding agent-written code in formal proofs rather than trusting agent behavior directly. | 4,921 | 492.1 |
| 49 | [Skills are the New SDKs - Elvin Aghammadzada, DataRobot](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LC3-P7v3yoI) | Agent skills | Elvin Aghammadzada argues that reusable skills are becoming the new packaging unit for agent capabilities, replacing SDKs. | 1,467 | 489.0 |
| 50 | [Video Has No Memory. Here's How We Built One. — James Le, TwelveLabs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mOf-PP4mVjA) | Video memory | James Le describes how TwelveLabs built persistent memory for video understanding. | 468 | 468.0 |
| 51 | [Beyond the Harness: A Journey Towards Adaptative Engineering - Rajiv Chandegra, Annicha Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qdZzND79mcg) | Adaptive engineering | Explores engineering systems that adapt beyond static evaluation harnesses. | 7,420 | 463.8 |
| 52 | [Computer-Use 2.0: Agents Just Got Multi-Cursor — Francesco Bonacci, Cua](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZSQb5fzRFPw) | Computer-use agents | Francesco Bonacci introduces multi-cursor computer-use agents that can act across several interfaces at once. | 3,686 | 460.8 |
| 53 | [Learned Execution Graphs for Anomaly Detection & Drift in APIs — Ritvik Pandya, JP Morgan Chase](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u1yaOeEX4e8) | API anomaly detection | Ritvik Pandya applies learned execution graphs to detect anomalies and drift in APIs. | 438 | 438.0 |
| 54 | [Design Patterns for AI Trust: Juries, Libraries, and Agent Tiers — Alex Bauer, Upside.tech](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YZQsWVeN3rE) | AI trust patterns | Presents design patterns like juries, libraries, and agent tiers for building trustworthy AI systems. | 4,967 | 413.9 |
| 55 | [Enterprise Agents Have a Structure Problem - Ishita Daga, Tesla](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B8l81jhvHbI) | Enterprise agents | Ishita Daga argues enterprise agents fail on structure and organization rather than model quality. | 1,189 | 396.3 |
| 56 | [How Autoresearch is changing ML research — Zhengyao Jiang, Weco](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iCj_ATyThvc) | Autonomous coding agents | Zhengyao Jiang recounts how an AI agent topped the leaderboard of OpenAI's hiring challenge. | 2,702 | 386.0 |
| 57 | [Local Agentic Theory For Mobile Games — Shafik Quoraishee & Joanne Song, The New York Times](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=418t26CVz-w) | On-device game agents | Covers running local agentic reasoning for mobile games at The New York Times. | 375 | 375.0 |
| 58 | [AI on Your Lakehouse: Context Comes in Shapes, Not Queries — Zach Blumenfeld, Neo4j](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kRkcNOsRyYg) | Lakehouse context | Zach Blumenfeld argues AI context on a lakehouse comes in graph shapes, not flat queries. | 373 | 373.0 |
| 59 | [Agentic Security: Permissions, Provenance, and the Agent Supply Chain — Steve Yegge, Gas Town](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yWS0udrIOc8) | Agent security | Steve Yegge covers permissions, provenance, and supply-chain risk for AI agents. | 1,068 | 356.0 |
| 60 | [Content Is Code - Matt Palmer, Conductor](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yv6xovSsB1U) | Content as code | Frames content itself as a form of code in AI-driven workflows. | 1,770 | 354.0 |
| 61 | [Autonomous Agents for Scientific Tasks - Sina Shahandeh, Radicait](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XLEYtv3cMlw) | Scientific agents | Sina Shahandeh describes building autonomous agents that carry out scientific research tasks. | 1,612 | 322.4 |
| 62 | [On AI and Knowledge — Pablo Castro, Distinguished Engineer & CVP for AI Knowledge, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGSFUqzqErE) | AI knowledge systems | Pablo Castro reflects on how AI reshapes the creation and use of organizational knowledge. | 1,929 | 321.5 |
| 63 | [Through the AI Fog: The Architectural Decision Agentic Security Depends On — Manoj Nair, Snyk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1EZdpEhwmNc) | Agentic security architecture | Argues one architectural decision determines whether agentic security holds up. | 953 | 317.7 |
| 64 | [Stop Renting Your Cognitive Infrastructure - Thiyagarajan Maruthavanan, Kalmantic Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Bck7ABCZRZI) | AI ownership | Advocates owning rather than renting the cognitive infrastructure behind AI systems. | 1,548 | 309.6 |
| 65 | [Should AI Engineers Still Read Code in 2026? The Z/L Continuum — Alex Volkov, ThursdAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZpK5PWX2YRM) | AI coding practice | Asks how much code AI engineers should still read as agents write more of it. | 3,962 | 304.8 |
| 66 | [Don't Let the LLM Drive - Ornella Bahidika & Joel Allou, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m24UKZomm7k) | Agent control flow | Makes the case for keeping deterministic control around the LLM rather than letting the model drive the whole workflow. | 884 | 294.7 |
| 67 | [Your Agents Need a Save Button - Hamza Tahir, ZenML](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bZISsg7H7DA) | Agent state persistence | Argues agents need durable checkpointing so their work can be saved and resumed reliably. | 1,459 | 291.8 |
| 68 | [The Pipeline Is Dead - Iris ten Teije, Sky Valley Ambient Computing](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bRnoEpoK5m4) | Agentic workflows | Reframes traditional software pipelines around more dynamic, ambient AI workflows. | 4,660 | 291.2 |
| 69 | [Stop AI Agent Hallucinations: 5 Techniques + Production Patterns - Elizabeth Fuentes, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vJukHCIv7Ck) | Agent hallucinations | Presents five production-tested techniques for reducing AI agent hallucinations. | 3,474 | 289.5 |
| 70 | [Teaching Coding Agents to do Spreadsheets - Nuno Campos, Witan Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HEFSExa0xl0) | Spreadsheet agents | Covers how to make coding agents useful for spreadsheet-heavy work. | 4,012 | 267.5 |
| 71 | [Building an ACP-Compatible Agent Live — Bennet Fenner, Zed](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HsxQICTLF84) | Agent protocols | A live build showing how an agent can integrate with the Agent Client Protocol ecosystem. | 3,962 | 264.1 |
| 72 | [Your LLM Stack Is a 2008 Database With Better Marketing — Lovina Dmello, NVIDIA](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XjI-AR4pt7Y) | LLM infrastructure | Argues today's LLM stacks resemble dated database systems dressed up with new marketing. | 787 | 262.3 |
| 73 | [Build AI Systems for Discernment, Not Approval - Angel Ortmann Lee, Duolingo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDqzWpwkSls) | AI product judgment | Advocates building AI systems that help users judge well instead of merely agreeing with them. | 4,108 | 256.8 |
| 74 | [From Tokens to Cells: Foundation Models for Single-Cell Biology - Akram Baharlouei, Altos Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-561cZmir5Q) | Single-cell biology models | Akram Baharlouei presents foundation models that treat single-cell biology data much like tokens in a language model. | 1,022 | 255.5 |
| 75 | [Using Spec-Driven Development for Production Workflows - Erik Hanchett, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IddXPepIAS4) | Spec-driven development | Shows how specifications can drive more reliable AI-assisted production workflows. | 6,372 | 254.9 |
| 76 | [Agents Need Receipts, Not More Tool Calls - Armanas Povilionis, Alithea Bio](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fu45geO3zX8) | Agent auditability | Argues agents should produce verifiable receipts of their actions rather than simply making more tool calls. | 1,272 | 254.4 |
| 77 | [Every Solo Agent Builder Eventually Reinvents a Worse Version of CI/CD - Sumaiya Shrabony](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WLXxTaPagA8) | Agent tooling | Argues solo builders end up rebuilding CI/CD-like infrastructure for their agents anyway. | 2,899 | 241.6 |
| 78 | [AI System Design: From Idea to Production - Apoorva Joshi, MongoDB](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T0HhO4YtTfE) | AI system design | Walks through moving an AI system from concept to production. | 6,025 | 241.0 |
| 79 | [RLM: Recursive Language Models for Large Codebases - Shashi, Superagentic AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8oyalrfwgjw) | Recursive language models | Introduces recursive language models as a technique for reasoning over very large codebases. | 2,614 | 237.6 |
| 80 | [The AI bugpocalypse is here. Now what? - Jack Cable, Corridor](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7JgIS42mz7U) | AI-generated code security | Argues that AI-written code is fueling a surge of security bugs and outlines how to respond. | 2,521 | 229.2 |
| 81 | [Building an Autonomous Engineering Org - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=whue9_YquGA) | Autonomous engineering | Discusses the organizational patterns behind autonomous AI-assisted engineering. | 5,488 | 219.5 |
| 82 | [MCP Apps: Primitives, discovery, and the Future of Software - Pietro Zullo, Manufact, Inc](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sAOBXCDiDOs) | MCP apps | Covers MCP app primitives, discovery, and how software interfaces may evolve. | 3,826 | 212.6 |
| 83 | [Agents Need Feature Flags - Sachin Gupta](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zU4EagB311U) | Agent rollout control | Makes the case for feature-flagging agent behavior so rollouts can be controlled safely. | 1,030 | 206.0 |
| 84 | [Your AI Product Will Fail Unless You Can Explain It - Veronica Hylak, Hey AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d_Ftrl3vfV0) | Explainability | Positions explanation as a core product requirement for successful AI systems. | 3,672 | 204.0 |
| 85 | [Build the AI GTM Agent That Knows the Buyer - Dr. Sajjan Kanukolanu, Position2 (Position Squared)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ltv-L5oMPIs) | Go-to-market agents | Shows how to build a go-to-market agent that understands the buyer well enough to drive real sales motions. | 608 | 202.7 |
| 86 | [The Agentic AI Engineer - Benedikt Sanftl, Mutagent](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pSto5YaNGUo) | Agentic engineering | Defines what changes when AI engineers work through agentic systems. | 4,861 | 202.5 |
| 87 | [We Gave an Agent Production Code Access and Then Tried to Sleep at Night — Moritz Johner, Form3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LqLoYksJ6do) | Agents in production | A candid account of granting an agent production code access and managing the risk. | 589 | 196.3 |
| 88 | [Your Voice Agent Doesn't Need a Frontier Model - Joel Allou & Ornella Bahidika, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fnLBmfsI_Fg) | Voice agents | Argues that voice agents can run effectively on smaller models rather than requiring a frontier model. | 586 | 195.3 |
| 89 | [Can Oncology Workflows Run Without Human Touch? - Anant Shankhdhar, Risa Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_cVfz88_j7A) | Healthcare agents | Anant Shankhdhar asks how far oncology workflows can be automated before a human must stay in the loop. | 563 | 187.7 |
| 90 | [You Didn't Ship a Bug. You Just Wrote It for a Human. - Ravi Madabhushi, Scalekit](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lMCxVorb9wM) | Human-facing bugs | Argues that agent mistakes shipped to users are still bugs, just paid for by a human instead of a compiler. | 737 | 184.2 |
| 91 | [Continual Learning for AI Agents: From Failures to Durable Improvements - Soheil Feizi, RELAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2IxD9OB3XuQ) | Continual learning | Looks at how agents can convert failures into durable improvements over time. | 3,244 | 180.2 |
| 92 | [It's 10pm. Do You Know Where Your Agents Are? — Kim Maida, Keycard](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I3znWC3MEXM) | Agent observability | Argues teams need real visibility and control over what their agents are doing. | 527 | 175.7 |
| 93 | [Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fWXJM-J0ZB8) | On-device AI | Covers frontier-quality AI results running closer to the device. | 4,200 | 175.0 |
| 94 | [What Does Done Even Mean? Agents and Paperclip's Liveness Model - Dotta, Paperclip](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7P0elyLIxXo) | Agent task completion | Proposes a liveness model for defining when an agent's task is actually complete. | 1,913 | 173.9 |
| 95 | [The Agentic Web and the Bazaar Era of AI - Ramesh Raskar, MIT Media Lab](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sum9DgexFRQ) | Agentic web | Frames the emerging agentic web as a decentralized "bazaar" era for AI-driven commerce and services. | 1,894 | 172.2 |
| 96 | [Agent Output Is Not UX: Rendering Layer Your LLM Pipeline Is Missing - Bala Ramdoss, Amazon Lens](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=maTp79FD9gI) | Agent UX rendering | Argues raw agent output is not UX and that a rendering layer is the missing piece in most LLM pipelines. | 515 | 171.7 |
| 97 | [Agents Building Agents - Alfonso Graziano, Nearform](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aHhB3sjGjkI) | Meta-agents | Explores agents that construct and orchestrate other agents. | 4,163 | 166.5 |
| 98 | [ReviewDebt: a practical framework for scoring every pull request — Sachin Gupta, Ebay](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TJPInBjhE4Q) | Code review process | Introduces a scoring framework for tracking review debt across pull requests. | 1,792 | 162.9 |
| 99 | [Your coding agent doesn't always follow your rules — Talha Sheikh, Checkout.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MpZzWMdmQCE) | Agent reliability | Discusses why coding agents drift from instructions and what that means for reliability. | 2,340 | 156.0 |
| 100 | [Privacy-Preserving Intelligence — Steve Korshakov, Bee (acq. Amazon)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IvE8n-ylFYY) | Privacy-preserving AI | Covers building intelligent systems that protect user privacy. | 465 | 155.0 |
| 101 | [Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=APh1Vx0oLmQ) | Agent infrastructure | Discusses infrastructure patterns for making stochastic agents more controllable. | 3,449 | 143.7 |
| 102 | [Voice Agents That Handle Interrupts - Chintan Agrawal and Daniel Wirjo, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hMlLw1LeIK8) | Voice agents | Covers how to build voice agents that gracefully handle user interruptions in real time. | 428 | 142.7 |
| 103 | [I Run a Fleet of AI Agents Across Three Machines. Here's What Broke. - Kyle Jaejun Lee, KRAFTON](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4kYl2_mqmnQ) | Multi-agent operations | Lessons from running multiple agents across machines and dealing with the operational failures. | 2,131 | 142.1 |
| 104 | [Semantic Blindness: 500,000 Sensors Confused an LLM - Raahul Singh & Vanč Levstik, Phaidra](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EUsPvBeIx70) | Sensor data / LLM limits | Explores how a massive sensor dataset exposed blind spots in an LLM's semantic understanding. | 1,559 | 141.7 |
| 105 | [The Missing Layer After Launch - Raphael Kalandadze, Wandero AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kZsf_Sfm7RU) | Post-launch AI systems | Discusses the operational/product layer needed after an AI product ships. | 2,485 | 138.1 |
| 106 | [Security Track Intro — Randall Degges, Snyk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2xJoimgoqBg) | Security track intro | Opens the security track with an overview of AI security themes. | 409 | 136.3 |
| 107 | [Agentic Development Security — Ezra Tanzer, Snyk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cgimkNGNjvU) | Agentic development security | Covers securing development workflows that rely on AI agents. | 406 | 135.3 |
| 108 | [Chat and citations won't save your vertical AI - Atul Ramachandran, Filed Inc](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGiXcVxSD3s) | Vertical AI product | Argues chat interfaces and citations alone aren't enough to make vertical AI products succeed. | 1,593 | 132.8 |
| 109 | [The Prompt Is Still a Punch Card - Ted Johnson, JoinIn AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hVJOnuhFmTA) | Prompting interfaces | Compares prompts to old low-level interfaces and suggests better abstractions are needed. | 2,785 | 132.6 |
| 110 | [The Factory That Dreams: 39 AI Agents, No Framework - Rushabh Doshi, Machinecraft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jtzh-GBXBWc) | Multi-agent systems | Describes running 39 AI agents together without relying on an agent framework. | 1,571 | 130.9 |
| 111 | [Think You Can Build a Game with AI? Think Again! - Danielle An & David Hoe, Meta](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=grdoOC1BT1s) | AI game development | A reality check on the limits and challenges of building games with AI. | 1,930 | 128.7 |
| 112 | [When Agents Meet Physical Data: The Other Physics of Agent Harnesses - Dmitry Petrov, DataChain](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bUJgirn4_yc) | Agent data infrastructure | Dmitry Petrov explores what happens when agent harnesses meet large, physical datasets. | 372 | 124.0 |
| 113 | [GTM Is You - Victoria Melnikova, Evil Martians](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G6IlDzj8OjA) | Go-to-market | A builder-oriented view of go-to-market where technical creators carry more of the motion. | 1,958 | 122.4 |
| 114 | [500 people vibe-coded for 30 days. I was one of them. - Sanja Grbic, Automattic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UcYoMg-8-L8) | Vibe coding | Lessons from a month-long large-scale vibe-coding experiment. | 1,924 | 120.2 |
| 115 | [A Song of Types and Agents - Roberto Stagi, Ratel](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UlFB6efYN5Q) | Type systems for agents | Looks at how strong typing can make agent-built software more reliable. | 1,311 | 119.2 |
| 116 | [remobi.app: Don't change your terminal workflow for mobile](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5192csoTkVo) | Mobile dev tools | Demos remobi.app, which brings your existing terminal workflow to mobile without changing it. | 1,236 | 112.4 |
| 117 | [Stop Evaluating Models Like It's the 50s - Alejandro Vidal, Mindmakers](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O3FEoMYvUf8) | Model evaluation | Argues for modernizing how AI models are evaluated beyond outdated benchmarks. | 1,071 | 107.1 |
| 118 | [Agents Need Receipts, Not More Tool Calls - Armanas Povilionis, Alithea Bio](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q9ycQHbDdJs) | Agent auditability | Argues agents should produce verifiable receipts of their actions rather than simply making more tool calls. | 316 | 105.3 |
| 119 | [How we taught agents to use good retrieval - Hanna Lichtenberg, Mixedbread AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1IdzkRVmWAA) | Agent retrieval | Explains approaches for making agents use retrieval more effectively. | 1,662 | 103.9 |
| 120 | [Develop at Idea Velocity - Jeffrey Lee-Chan, Snapchat](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9arM9b7JgOo) | Development speed | Makes the case for building fast enough to keep pace with ideas rather than process. | 1,159 | 96.6 |
| 121 | [Claws Out: Securing and Building with OpenClaw - Nick Taylor, Pomerium](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xg1zNlzw7Jk) | OpenClaw security | Covers security considerations for building on and with the OpenClaw ecosystem. | 1,086 | 90.5 |
| 122 | [Structuring the Unstructured - Cedric Clyburn, Red Hat](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-x5GEVnkuRw) | Data structuring | Covers techniques for turning unstructured information into usable structured data. | 2,233 | 89.3 |
| 123 | [Your agent is blindfolded — Johan Lajili, Poolside AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iRcX54EO5g8) | Agent observability | Argues agents need better visibility into their environment to act effectively. | 1,318 | 87.9 |
| 124 | [SWE-Marathon: Evaluating Coding Agents at Billion-Token Scale - Rishi Desai, Abundant AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rx8f05JI_WA) | Coding-agent evals | Presents large-scale evaluation of coding agents over very long token horizons. | 1,306 | 81.6 |
| 125 | [Medic for Apache Spark - First Aid for Failing Jobs - Drasko Profirovic, Pinterest](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0RNNfxpdbQk) | Data pipeline debugging | Drasko Profirovic presents a tool that diagnoses and repairs failing Apache Spark jobs. | 240 | 80.0 |
| 126 | [Your Agent Failed in Prod. Good Luck Reproducing It. - Tisha Chawla & Susheem Koul, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Lc8zRh9muoY) | Production debugging | Covers the reproducibility challenge when production agents fail. | 1,908 | 79.5 |
| 127 | [Running a Chess YouTube Channel entirely by AI — Stephan Steinfurt, TNG](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BqZrTdgBaPw) | AI media automation | Describes using AI to automate an entire chess YouTube channel workflow. | 1,063 | 70.9 |
| 128 | [Your Agent Is Wasting Tokens and You Don't Know It - Erik Hanchett, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uiP88SpCi1Q) | Token efficiency | Highlights hidden token waste in agent systems and ways to reduce it. | 1,725 | 69.0 |
| 129 | [You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6bmM45jkMDY) | Human skills | Argues for the continued importance of human facilitation and social skill. | 1,643 | 68.5 |
| 130 | [The Prompt is the Platform - Dominik Tornow, Resonate HQ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DqtmZE6Hl0g) | Prompt platforms | Frames prompts as a platform layer for AI applications and workflows. | 1,608 | 67.0 |
| 131 | [Using RL Agent to Detect and Remediate ETL Pipeline Failures - Anna Marie Benzon](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LrGCT7G_rU8) | RL for data pipelines | Shows reinforcement-learning agents applied to ETL failure detection and remediation. | 1,608 | 67.0 |
| 132 | [Browser Agents Don't Need Better Models. They Need Better Eyes. - Kushan Raj, ARK](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JnubYCYunk8) | Browser agents | Argues browser agents are bottlenecked by perception, not model quality. | 1,587 | 63.5 |
| 133 | [Respect The Process - Andrew Dumit, Watershed Technology Inc.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CLttOU7n6sI) | Engineering process | Emphasizes process discipline in AI-enabled engineering workflows. | 925 | 57.8 |
| 134 | [Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=65X0pQ6Lmbg) | Multimodal UX | Covers challenges and promise in voice-to-visual AI workflows. | 1,352 | 54.1 |
| 135 | [OpenClaw in Your Hand: Building a Physical AI Terminal - Lech Kalinowski, Callstack](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=akk6KRlcwW4) | Physical AI devices | Shows a handheld or physical AI terminal concept built around OpenClaw. | 1,199 | 48.0 |
| 136 | [AI-Driven Multi-Document Correlation for Financial Compliance - Varsha Shah, Independent](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Iwe_RY-fYgI) | Compliance AI | Applies AI to correlating multiple documents for financial compliance workflows. | 1,195 | 47.8 |
| 137 | [Your LLM Deception Monitor Is Broken. The Fix Is in the Training Data - Sachin Kumar, LexisNexis](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IQkVMvXQKLY) | Safety monitoring | Argues that deception monitoring depends heavily on training-data quality. | 680 | 45.3 |
| 138 | [User Signal Dies at the Retrieval Boundary - Sonam Pankaj, StarlightSearch](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jx4ZFEAq6bY) | Retrieval signals | Discusses preserving user intent and signals across retrieval system boundaries. | 925 | 37.0 |
| 139 | [When All Context Matters: Extended Cache Augmented Generation - Luis Romero-Sevilla, Orbis](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XovaGv4f39A) | Cache-augmented generation | Covers using extended cache techniques when broad context matters. | 894 | 35.8 |
| 140 | [Using LLMs to Secure Source Code — Eugene Yan, Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=imFedndyXYQ) | Code security | Eugene Yan shows how LLMs can find and fix source-code vulnerabilities. | 202 | 33.7 |
| 141 | [Bypassing the Multimodal Tax: Hybrid RAG, SQL RRF & UI Telemetry - Abed Matini, Ogilvy](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Akm1sqvWG4A) | Hybrid retrieval | Combines RAG, SQL, reciprocal-rank fusion, and UI telemetry for multimodal workflows. | 839 | 33.6 |
| 142 | [Research to Reality: Bringing Frontier ML Research to Production - Vaidas Razgaitis, Higharc](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OXMMN-XbxwA) | ML productionization | Discusses translating frontier ML research into production systems. | 838 | 33.5 |

## Code to Replicate the Ranking

This script uses [`yt-dlp`](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp) to fetch the channel's current video metadata, filters to recent posted conference talks, and ranks by views per day. Install `yt-dlp` first if needed:

```bash
uv tool install yt-dlp
```

Then run:

```python
import datetime as dt
import json
import subprocess

CHANNEL_URL = "https://www.youtube.com/@aiDotEngineer/videos"
REFERENCE_DATE = dt.date(2026, 7, 23)
PLAYLIST_END = 120

EXCLUDE_TITLE_SUBSTRINGS = [
    "Vibe Reel",
    "Things to Know about AIE",
]

cmd = [
    "yt-dlp",
    "--playlist-end",
    str(PLAYLIST_END),
    "--skip-download",
    "--ignore-errors",
    "--print",
    "%()j",
    CHANNEL_URL,
]

proc = subprocess.run(
    cmd,
    check=False,
    text=True,
    capture_output=True,
    timeout=420,
)

rows = []
for line in proc.stdout.splitlines():
    if not line.startswith("{"):
        continue

    video = json.loads(line)
    title = video.get("title") or ""
    upload_date = video.get("upload_date")
    view_count = video.get("view_count")

    if not upload_date or view_count is None:
        continue
    if upload_date < "20260608":
        continue
    if any(skip in title for skip in EXCLUDE_TITLE_SUBSTRINGS):
        continue

    uploaded = dt.datetime.strptime(upload_date, "%Y%m%d").date()
    days_since_upload = max(1, (REFERENCE_DATE - uploaded).days)
    views_per_day = view_count / days_since_upload

    rows.append(
        {
            "title": title,
            "url": video.get("webpage_url")
            or f"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={video.get('id')}",
            "upload_date": uploaded.isoformat(),
            "views": view_count,
            "days_since_upload": days_since_upload,
            "views_per_day": views_per_day,
            "duration_minutes": round((video.get("duration") or 0) / 60, 1),
        }
    )

rows.sort(key=lambda row: row["views_per_day"], reverse=True)

print("| Rank | Video | Uploaded | Views | Days | Views/day |")
print("|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|")

for rank, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
    print(
        "| {rank} | [{title}]({url}) | {upload_date} | "
        "{views:,} | {days_since_upload} | {views_per_day:,.1f} |".format(
            rank=rank,
            **row,
        )
    )
```

A couple of notes:

- `--ignore-errors` lets the script skip scheduled premieres that do not have watchable metadata yet.
- `PLAYLIST_END = 120` was enough for this snapshot because the recent conference batch appeared in the first 120 channel videos.
- The views are a snapshot. Re-running the script later will produce different view counts and a different views/day ranking.
